title: Podman Socket API exceeds file name limit when passing seccomp profile, where Docker would succeed
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using Podman to run Nextcloud AIO, which uses the Docker Socket API to manage containers. One of the containers it manages, Collabora, supports using a seccomp profile to reduce permissions necessary (and make it runnable in rootless). However, when Nextcloud attempts to create the container through the socket, the following error is returned:

```
2025-11-29T20:46:37Z Message: Could not create container nextcloud-aio-collabora: {"cause":"file name too long","message":"container create: opening seccomp profile failed: open {
  "defaultAction": "SCMP_ACT_ERRNO",
  "defaultErrnoRet": 1,
  "archMap": [
    {
      "architecture": "SCMP_ARCH_X86_64",
      "subArchitectures": [

        "SCMP_ARCH_X32"

```

This does not happen in Docker. From what upstream has stated (https://github.com/nextcloud/all-in-one/discussions/3487#discussioncomment-15171660), the whole seccomp file contents are passed in the request body in the socket API, so perhaps Podman is creating some temp file and exceeding the maximum file length?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a container (with access to the Podman socket) that creates another container with a seccomp profile. Beyond that, I'm not sure, maybe the container itself has to have a long name?

### Describe the results you received

An error when applying the seccomp profile that the file name is too long

### Describe the results you expected

No error

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

This is running on Ubuntu 24.04 on Hetzner cloud

### Additional information

This may require long container names? I'm not exactly sure what goes on under the hood when a seccomp profile is passed

comments.body:
this is podman 4.x.  Podman 5.x has been out for nearly 2 years.  Can you verify this is still an issue with the latest Podman release or from main branch?

if that fails, please consider trying to give us a simple reproducer as that will help a lot.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
btw, if it still reproduces, i suspect you might be right.

> this is podman 4.x. Podman 5.x has been out for nearly 2 years. Can you verify this is still an issue with the latest Podman release or from main branch?
> 
> if that fails, please consider trying to give us a simple reproducer as that will help a lot.

I'm on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS for my server, but I'll see if I can recreate the issue locally on my laptop

I'm having a hard time reproducing this issue on both my server (Podman 4.X) and laptop (Podman 5.X) with the script attached below (using `podman-py`). The most I get is Podman on my server hanging when creating the container, and Podman on my laptop just closing the connection with no response; neither case returns the seccomp error.

Of course, this script is based on the assumption that the container name causes the issue, which is just a guess.
 
```py
# Recreate a bug in Podman where applying
# seccomp profiles causes a file name limit
# error.
#
# This was discovered on an Ubuntu 24.04 server
# using the ext4 filesystem, and this script was
# deveoped on an Arch Linux laptop using the BTRFS
# filesystem, which should have the same limits
# with file names.

import json
from podman import PodmanClient
from podman.domain.images import Image

NAME = "A" * (4096 * 50000)


def main() -> None:
    seccomp_profile: str | None = None
    with open("./seccomp_profile.json", "r") as f:
        seccomp_profile = json.dumps(json.load(f))

    with PodmanClient() as client:
        if not client.ping():
            print(f"Service not found: {client.api.base_url}")

        print("Pulling Hello Image")
        image = client.images.pull("quay.io/podman/hello", tag="latest")

        assert isinstance(image, Image)

        print("Creating container...")

        container = client.containers.create(
            image=image,
            name=NAME,
            security_opt=[
                "label:disable",
                f"seccomp={seccomp_profile}",
                "no-new-privileges=true",
            ],
        )
        print(container)

        assert len(NAME) == len(container.name)

        print("Removing container...")
        container.remove(force=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

I am having the same issue on CentOS Stream 10 with latest updates. 

```
root@cloud:~# podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.6.0
API Version:   5.6.0
Go Version:    go1.24.6 (Red Hat 1.24.6-1.el10)
Git Commit:    56f1962fb0af60c29c7c5d27bdb4f26b13e69afe
Built:         Fri Aug 22 02:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  builder@centos.org
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

The error is happening when I try to launch Nextcloud AIO, which in turn uses the mounted podman socket to launch collabora (where I get the error).

```
Dec 20 08:04:00 cloud nextcloud-aio-mastercontainer[2708434]: Fatal error: Uncaught Exception: Could not create container nextcloud-aio-collabora: [{"cause":"file name too long","message":"c>](io-collabora: {"cause":"file name too long","message":"container create: opening seccomp profile failed: open)
```

I use the following Quadlet to launch nextcloud-aio:

```
[Unit]
Description=Nextcloud AIO Master Container

[Container]
ContainerName=nextcloud-aio-mastercontainer
Image=ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/all-in-one:latest
Volume=/var/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Volume=nextcloud_aio_mastercontainer:/mnt/docker-aio-config
Volume=/opt/nextcloud-aio/data:/data
Environment=APACHE_PORT=13080
Environment=NEXTCLOUD_DATADIR="/opt/nextcloud-aio/data"
PublishPort=127.0.0.1:8080:8080
PublishPort=127.0.0.1:8443:8443

[Service]
# Restart service when sleep finishes
Restart=always
ExecStartPost=podman exec -it nextcloud-aio-mastercontainer sudo -u www-data php /var/www/docker-aio/php/src/Cron/StartAndUpdateContainers.php

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
DefaultInstance=100
```

Once Nextcloud is running, connect to the AIO interface on port 8080 via HTTPS and accept the certificate. Enable the "Collabora" container and try to start the containers.

Same issue as what @InfoSec812 is seeing.  Podman version 5.4.2 on Debian 13.  Quadlet:

```
[Container]
ContainerName=nextcloud-aio-mastercontainer
Image=ghcr.io/nextcloud-releases/all-in-one:latest
AutoUpdate=registry

Environment=APACHE_PORT=11000
Environment=APACHE_ADDITIONAL_BINDING=127.0.0.1
Environment=SKIP_DOMAIN_VALIDATION=true

Network=frontend.network

# PublishPort=80:80
PublishPort=28080:8080
# PublishPort=8443:8443

Volume=nextcloud.volume:/mnt/docker-aio-config
Volume=%t/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z

Label=traefik.enable=true
Label=traefik.http.routers.nextcloud.rule=Host(`nextcloud.domain.tld`) # not really domain.tld
Label=traefik.http.services.nextcloud.loadbalancer.server.port=80

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStartSec=90

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

I **DID** find a sort of workaround to disable SECCOMP for Collabora, but this degrades the security of the system. 

Adding the following to my Quadlet allows it to work:

```
Environment=COLLABORA_SECCOMP_DISABLED=true
```

Disabling seccomp is not safe.
This should be fixed in podman.
But it can only be reproduced when setting up the nextcloud aio master container and launching Collabora from there.

Same issue here. Fedora 42, Podman 5.7.1, rootful setup. AIO fails to create the Collabora container with the same "file name too long" error when passing the seccomp profile through the socket.

I've been working around it with `COLLABORA_SECCOMP_DISABLED=true`, but worth noting that AIO won't let you enable Fulltextsearch when that flag is set. So you end up having to pick between Collabora and full-text search, basically blocking a core feature entirely
I have this issue with Rocky9 and Podman 5.6 and Nextcloud's Collabora container.
